name: White Chocolate Creme Brulee
recipeIngredient: 3 oz White chocolate - imported, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F and place a rack in the center.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together egg yolks and 1/4 cup sugar until pale.
3. In a heavy saucepan, bring cream and remaining 1/4 cup sugar to a simmer.
4. Reduce heat to low and gradually add the chopped chocolate to the cream mixture, stirring until smooth.
5. Gradually whisk the hot chocolate mixture into the egg yolk mixture.
6. Stir in vanilla extract.
7. Pour the custard into four 10-oz. custard cups (or creme brulee cups) and place them in a large baking pan.
8. Add enough hot water to the pan to cover about halfway up the sides of the cups.
9. Bake until the custards are set in the center, about 1 hour.
10. Remove the custards from the water and let cool completely.
11. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
12. Preheat the broiler.
13. Sprinkle 2 tablespoons sugar over each custard.
14. Broil until the sugar caramelizes, watching carefully, about 2 minutes per cup.
15. Serve immediately or refrigerate up to 1 hour and serve cold.
